servicesSection.title: From research to real work,
servicesSection.desc: Automation that doesn't stop at a demo. Four ways Codium Lab can collaborate with you.

items.title: AI Automation Consulting
items.summary: Workflow diagnosis → automation design → production handoff
items.body: We analyze the texture of repetitive work to find exactly where LLMs, RPA, and scripts fit best. We design automation that reaches production, not just proof-of-concept.

items.title: Custom LLM Tool Development
items.summary: A dedicated assistant tailored to your organization's data
items.body: We design search, summarization, and generation tools based on your internal docs, manuals, and customer data, then deploy them in compliance with your security policies.

items.title: AI Literacy Training
items.summary: Practical training programs for teams and individuals
items.body: Through the Adium brand, we deliver immediately applicable AI education — from executive workshops to hands-on practitioner sessions.

items.title: Digital Goods Collaboration
items.summary: AI design brought into everyday objects
items.body: The Badium brand transforms AI design into goods and content. Brand and campaign-scale collaboration available.

processSection.title: From discovery to handoff,
processSection.desc: Divided into short, clear stages so it's always visible what gets decided when.

items.title: Discover
items.body: We start with on-site interviews and data flow observation. This is the stage where we redefine the problem.

items.title: Design
items.body: We place users, models, data, and operational cost on one canvas and sketch the simplest solution.

items.title: Build
items.body: Production-ready code, not prototypes. Monitoring and rollback scenarios are built in from the start.

items.title: Transfer
items.body: We hand over documentation, training, and management tools so the organization can operate independently. No dependencies left behind.

faqSection.title: questions
faqSection.desc: For questions not listed here, use the contact form below. We reply within 1–2 business days.

items.q: What size companies do you collaborate with?
items.a: From startups to mid-sized enterprises. That said, 'Is the problem well-defined?' and 'Can decision-makers be involved?' are more important variables for successful collaboration.
Remote collaboration is our default. On-site workshops or interviews at our Gwangmyeong office are conducted offline only when necessary.

items.q: Should we start with education or consulting?
items.a: If AI adoption is low in your organization, we recommend starting with Adium's literacy training. If you have a clear workflow to automate, consulting first. We'll decide together in a free diagnostic meeting.

items.q: Can you work in environments with strict security policies?
items.a: Yes. We configure for closed internal networks, on-premise LLMs, and VPC isolation. We create a security review checklist together before collaboration begins.

items.q: Can we do a short PoC only?
items.a: Absolutely. We typically propose a 2–4 week discovery + PoC package first. At this stage, we jointly judge whether this automation is truly needed.

brands.metaDesc: Codium Lab's two brands — Adium (AI literacy education) and Badium (digital goods studio).
brands.title: Two distinct brands
brands.desc: To bring Codium Lab's research into everyday life, we operate two sister brands. Adium elevates human learning; Badium elevates human aesthetics.

subBrands.description: An AI literacy education brand where AI and learning meet to elevate everyday intelligence. From executive workshops to practitioner hands-ons and full school curricula.
A digital goods studio that bakes the sensibility of AI design into warm, everyday objects. Handles brand collaboration, campaign seasonal goods, and illustration licensing.

whyTwo.title: Education and design are different paths
whyTwo.body: There are two ways AI reaches people — head (knowledge & skills) and hands (sensibility & aesthetics). We don't keep both paths in a single organization.

whyTwo.title: Each brand has a clear audience
whyTwo.body: Adium serves organizations and learners; Badium serves brand and campaign teams. Sharing one entry point blurs both audiences.

whyTwo.title: Codium Lab backs both
whyTwo.body: Technology, LLM, and automation for both brands is owned by Codium Lab. The brands can focus purely on their respective audiences.

collab.title: Education + Design together
collab.body: An integrated package: elevate AI literacy internally while sending messages externally through campaigns and goods.

collab.title: Brand-by-brand consulting
collab.body: Solo Adium education adoption or solo Badium design collaboration are both possible. Coordination with Codium Lab is included.

process.metaDesc: Codium Lab's four-stage collaboration — Discover → Design → Build → Transfer.
process.title: From discovery to handoff,
process.desc: Collaboration in four short, clear stages. What gets decided when, and which deliverables move to the next stage — all agreed upon before we begin.
process.agreementsDesc: Operating principles we agree on before starting to keep collaboration smooth.

steps.title: Discover
steps.body: We start with on-site interviews and data flow observation. First, we determine whether there's really a place for AI.

steps.title: Design
steps.body: We place users, models, data, and operational cost on one canvas and sketch the simplest solution. Complexity is a cost.

steps.title: Build
steps.body: Production-ready code, not prototypes. Monitoring and rollback scenarios built in from the start.

steps.title: Transfer
steps.body: We hand over documentation, training, and management tools so the organization can operate independently. The goal is to leave no dependencies.

agreements.title: Weekly review
agreements.body: At a fixed time each week, we jointly summarize progress, decisions, and blockers.

agreements.title: Shared channel
agreements.body: Work, decisions, and documentation are centralized in one Slack/Teams channel.

agreements.title: Decision log
agreements.body: Major decisions are captured in short notes. Six months later, you can still see who decided what and why.

agreements.title: Clean handoff
agreements.body: Designed so no additional work is needed after the transfer stage. Follow-up coaching is negotiated separately.

faq.metaDesc: Codium Lab collaboration FAQ — general, security, timeline, operations, handoff.
faq.title: Frequently asked
faq.desc: Questions we frequently hear before collaboration begins. For questions not listed here, use the contact form — we reply within 1–2 business days.
faq.ctaLabel: Ask a question not listed here

items.q: What size companies do you work with?
items.a: From startups to mid-sized enterprises. That said, 'Is the problem well-defined?' and 'Can decision-makers be involved?' are more important variables for successful collaboration.
Remote is our default. On-site workshops or interviews at our Gwangmyeong office are conducted offline only when necessary.

items.q: Should we start with education or consulting?
items.a: If AI adoption is low, start with Adium's literacy training. If you have a clear workflow to automate, consulting first. We'll decide together in a free diagnostic meeting.

items.q: Can you work in strict security environments?
items.a: Yes. We configure for closed internal networks, on-premise LLMs, and VPC isolation. We create a security review checklist together before collaboration begins.

items.q: Is customer data used for model training?
items.a: No. Our core design principle is 'no training on customer data.' Model settings and data flows are documented from the start.

items.q: Can you integrate with existing systems?
items.a: We can integrate with all standard tools: Slack, Jira, Notion, Confluence, GitHub, internal wikis. Internal systems proceed after API/DB schema agreement.

items.q: Can we run just a short PoC?
items.a: Absolutely. We typically propose a 2–4 week discovery + PoC package first. At this stage, we jointly judge whether this automation is truly needed.
By scope, duration, and team size. Instead of a fixed price list, we negotiate based on agreed deliverables. The initial diagnostic meeting is free.
Depends on team availability. We review the schedule honestly in the diagnostic meeting and tell you clearly when something isn't feasible.

items.q: Can our team operate the system after implementation?
items.a: Handoff is part of our scope. This includes an operations manual, coaching, and optional in-house trainer development — with no dependencies left behind.

items.q: Can we handle model or prompt changes ourselves?
items.a: We hand over prompt versioning, testing, and monitoring systems. Model update impact assessment procedures are also documented.

steps.title: Send an inquiry
steps.body: Describe the problem you want to solve, the outcome you expect, and your timing — freely.

steps.title: 30-min diagnostic meeting
steps.body: Conducted remotely (Google Meet). A relaxed first conversation.

steps.title: Collaboration proposal
steps.body: We present scope, timeline, and cost within one week. You decide here whether to proceed.

form.nameMax: Name must be 60 characters or fewer.
form.orgMax: Organization must be 80 characters or fewer.
form.emailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address.
form.messagePlaceholder: Describe the problem you'd like to solve, the result you expect, and your timeline.
form.messageMin: Message must be at least 10 characters.
form.messageMax: Message must be 2000 characters or fewer.
form.successMsg: Your inquiry has been received. We'll reply soon.
form.privacyNote: Stored in a SQLite-compatible DB upon submission and not shared externally.

ai.metaDesc: Codium Lab's AI approach: build with AI, embed it in workflow. Claude, Cursor, MCP, RAG-based organizational adoption.
ai.heroEyebrow: AI · A new way to build and operate
ai.heroDesc: Codium Lab doesn't position AI as just a helpful tool. Code line by line, meeting by meeting, operation cycle by cycle — we follow the texture of human work and naturally weave AI into each step.
ai.pillarsDesc: The place where things are built (development) and the place where work happens (workflow). Codium Lab's approach is to weave AI naturally into both.
ai.stackDesc: Not locked into any single vendor — we combine the right tools for each purpose.
ai.patternsDesc: We don't just attach results. Discover → Prototype → Integrate → Operate, four stages, built together.
ai.casesDesc: Adoptable scenarios — start small, reach production.
ai.harnessDesc: The moment an issue arrives, the pipeline wakes. An orchestrator coordinates every step, AI routes by task type, and the skill engine executes. People only step in at approval gates.

harnessCapabilities.label: AutoPilot
harnessCapabilities.body: Detects issues via JQL and automatically triggers the AI pipeline. Registered as a macOS LaunchAgent daemon.

harnessCapabilities.label: Scheduler
harnessCapabilities.body: Runs skills and workflows on a Cron schedule. AI generates Cron expressions from plain language.

harnessCapabilities.label: Approval Gates
harnessCapabilities.body: Every planning and output stage requires human review and approval before proceeding. Control lives inside automation.

harnessCapabilities.label: Live Monitor
harnessCapabilities.body: Every session, tool, and Sub-Agent run is visible live on the dashboard via WebSocket.

ai.closingTitle: A place for AI in your team —
ai.closingDesc: We'll check together in a free diagnostic meeting whether your team truly has a place where AI is needed.
ai.closingCtaPrimary: Apply for a free diagnostic meeting

pillars.title: We build with AI
pillars.summary: Claude · Cursor · MCP — as fast as possible within model limits.
pillars.body: We weave AI agents, code automation, and test generation into the everyday development cycle to shorten the time from PoC to production. Models are tools — decisions are made by people and domain.
items: Standardized pair programming with Claude Code · Codex · Cursor
Multi-Model routing (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) — cost vs. quality separation
Connecting internal APIs/docs/schemas to models via MCP servers

pillars.title: We build systems that embed in workflow
pillars.summary: AI at the center of workflow, in a place that elevates people.
pillars.body: Slack · Jira · Notion · internal wikis — without interrupting the existing flow of work, we plant RAG, agents, and automation bots inside it. So AI tools become 'colleagues you work with,' not 'tools you use.'
items: Internal document RAG assistant (meeting notes, manuals, product specs)
Auto analysis, summarization & triage bots for Jira/Linear/Slack
Custom LLM tools based on customer support & CS data
Observability AI combined with operational log & issue dashboards

patterns.title: Discovery
patterns.body: We interview and observe the team's actual workflow. We first judge whether there's really a place for AI, and whether it should go there.

patterns.title: Prototype
patterns.body: We build a small slice assuming production in 1–2 weeks and validate it with real-use data. A live version, not a demo.

patterns.title: Integrate
patterns.body: We design AI tools to go inside existing tools (Slack, Jira, internal systems), so users don't need to open a separate screen.

patterns.title: Operate
patterns.body: Prompts, models, and data all change over time. We hand over monitoring, rollback, and retraining cycles to the operations team.

cases.title: Spec → Code → Verify Automation
cases.body: When a Jira issue comes in, multiple models generate specs, the best one is adopted, and a pipeline runs automatically through code → test → verification.

cases.title: Internal Wiki RAG Assistant
cases.body: Embed scattered meeting notes, manuals, and CS logs, then ask questions in natural language from Slack. Sources are always verified with original links.
cases.meta: Vector DB · Slack Bot · Security isolation

cases.title: Issue Triage Bot
cases.body: Issues posted to CS and operations channels are automatically classified, summarized, and tagged, then routed to the right person. Less volume for humans to read; decisions stay with people.

principles.title: Human-in-the-loop
principles.body: AI is never given a seat where it decides alone. A human always reviews last and presses last.

principles.title: Secure by default
principles.body: Designed to meet security requirements: internal networks, VPC, on-premise LLMs. Data flow is defined first.

principles.title: Operable, not magical
principles.body: We take responsibility for the cost of changing prompts, models, and tools. Production-ready systems, not magic demos.

game.metaDesc: Play web games made by Codium Lab directly in your browser. Snake Grow Battle — a battle royale snake game.
game.hubDesc: Play games made by Codium Lab directly in your browser.
game.feedbackText: Have a game idea or feedback?
game.appBannerDesc: Everything about snake battle — 9 game modes, skin synthesis, and skill system, all in one app.
game.appBannerWebNote: The games below are web previews of the app modes

snake.subtitle: Battle Royale Snake
snake.description: Control a snake with your mouse, eat food to grow. Hit another snake's body and it's game over. Be the last snake standing in the shrinking storm zone.

airplane.subtitle: Battle Royale Air Combat
airplane.description: Control a fighter jet with your mouse and shoot down enemies. Bullets fire automatically — reduce enemy HP to zero to destroy them. Be the last ace in the shrinking storm zone.
controls: Mouse move → aim (fighter auto-rotates toward mouse)

bubble.subtitle: Bubble Shooter
bubble.description: Connect 3 or more same-color bubbles to pop them. Use special bubbles (⭐row clear, 💣bomb, 🌈rainbow) to trigger FEVER mode. Clear all bubbles in 80 shots to win!

defense.subtitle: Tower Defense
defense.description: Summon and merge snake towers to defend the island. Use gacha to summon C~MAX tier towers, drag same-tier towers to merge them. Survive 100 waves to win!

damon.name: Damon
damon.desc: 20-year full-stack developer. Researches AI vibe-coding, harness building, and automation system design. Handles IT development and AI consulting — and ships Android games and iOS hybrid apps.

nari.name: Emily
nari.desc: Former film power blogger with deep expertise in blog and SNS content strategy. Creates AI-generated videos for YouTube and Instagram, and runs elementary school AI education programs.
